title: Epic: Migrate xTerior to Julia/Python
description: This epic tracks the migration of the `xTerior` module from Wolfram `xAct` to a new implementation in Julia with a Python wrapper. `xTerior` provides tools for exterior calculus, differential forms, and related operations.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] All functionality of the `xTerior` module is ported to a new Julia implementation.
- [ ] A comprehensive Python wrapper for the Julia `xTerior` implementation is created.
- [ ] The new implementation passes all Layer 1 (Unit) tests extracted from the `xTerior` documentation notebooks.
- [ ] The new implementation passes all relevant Layer 2 (Property) tests for exterior algebra and calculus.
- [ ] The new implementation's performance on Layer 3 (Performance) benchmarks is within 5x of the Wolfram `xAct` baseline.
- [ ] The Python wrapper passes all tests, with performance overhead not exceeding 2x of the direct Julia execution time.

title: Phase 2: Result Envelope & Normalization
description:
Create Result dataclass and normalization pipeline.

## Result Envelope
- src/sxact/oracle/result.py - Result dataclass (status, type, repr, normalized, properties, diagnostics, error)
- Update src/sxact/oracle/client.py - Return Result objects

## Normalization Pipeline
- src/sxact/normalize/__init__.py
- src/sxact/normalize/pipeline.py - normalize() function with:
  1. Whitespace normalization
  2. Dummy index canonicalization ($1, $2, ...)
  3. Term ordering (lexicographic)
  4. Coefficient normalization

## Tests (TDD)
- tests/normalize/__init__.py
- tests/normalize/test_pipeline.py

## Success Criteria
- T[-a, -b] → T[-$1, -$2]
- B + A → A + B
- 2*x → 2 x
- uv run pytest tests/normalize/ passes

title: Analyze xCore API: identify minimal function set needed by xTensor and xPerm
description: Read resources/xAct/xCore/xCore.m in full and cross-reference against resources/xAct/xTensor/xTensor.m and resources/xAct/xPerm/xPerm.m to identify which xCore symbols are actually imported and used by downstream packages. Categorize every public xCore symbol as: (A) must-implement (used by xTensor or xPerm), (B) stdlib-equivalent (direct Julia or Python native replaces it, no implementation needed), or (C) skip (Wolfram notebook display only, e.g. string formatting utilities). Output: add the categorized function list as notes on sxAct-8wd before marking this ticket complete.
close_reason: Categorized all 63 public xCore symbols (34 must-implement A, 2 stdlib-equivalent B, 29 skip C); notes added to sxAct-8wd

title: doc-004: Write Getting Started Guide
close_reason: Wrote Getting Started guide with accurate code examples from the real API: OracleClient, evaluate_with_xact, normalize pipeline, three-tier comparator, and test example

title: Implement programming utilities in Julia (list ops, options, linking, dagger)
description: Implement Julia equivalents of xCore programming utility functions that are either thin wrappers or have no direct stdlib replacement. Based on must-implement and stdlib-equivalent categories from sxAct-0q8. Covers: (1) list utilities: just_one, map_if_plus, thread_array, (2) option utilities: check_options, true_or_false, report_set, report_set_option, (3) symbol linking: symbol_join, link_symbols, unlink_symbol, (4) dagger utilities: has_dagger_q, make_dagger_symbol. Where Julia stdlib is a direct replacement, document the mapping in a comment and do not implement a wrapper. Unit tests for all non-trivial implementations.

title: Implement PythonAdapter stub (returns not-implemented)
description: Implement PythonAdapter: a concrete TestAdapter (sxAct-hij) that stubs all interface methods by returning a Result with status=not_implemented. Purpose: mirrors JuliaAdapter stub, validates the adapter interface from the Python wrapper perspective, ensures the interface design works for a Python-native implementation. Must pass the adapter conformance test suite defined in sxAct-hij.
close_reason: Stubs implemented at src/sxact/adapter/{julia,python}_stub.py; 24 conformance tests each pass.

title: Write Layer 1 unit tests and Layer 2 property tests for Julia xCore
description: Write Layer 1 unit tests and Layer 2 property tests for the Julia xCore implementation. L1 tests: deterministic tests in tests/unit/test_xcore_julia.py or src/julia/test/runtests.jl, targeting over 90 percent coverage of the public Julia XCore API. L2 tests: property-based tests for symbol registry invariants (no two packages register the same name, validate_symbol always throws on collisions, hook registration ordering preserved). TOML-format xCore tests from sxAct-4kw run via the TOML runner from sxAct-z8g. All L1 tests must pass without the oracle running.

title: Implement extension mechanism (xTension/MakexTensions) in Julia
description: Implement the Julia equivalent of xCore extension mechanism (xTension and MakexTensions). Background: xTension lets packages register hooks that fire at the Beginning or End of def-commands like DefMetric, without modifying the original package source. In Julia this should be a hook registry: xtension(package, defcommand, moment, fn) registers fn, and make_xtensions(defcommand, moment, args...) fires all registered hooks for that command-moment combination. Source reference: resources/xAct/xCore/xCore.m line 606. Unit tests must verify hooks fire in registration order and at both Beginning and End moments.

title: Epic: Test Harness Infrastructure (Sprint 2-4)
description: Covers Sprint 2-4 of the test harness roadmap (specs/2026-01-22-design-framework-gaps.md Phase 6). Delivers: TOML-based test file format with JSON Schema, adapter interface with Wolfram/Julia/Python implementations, per-file test isolation, xact-test CLI runner, and oracle snapshot generation and comparison for CI use. Completion unblocks validation of any Julia or Python xAct implementation against the Wolfram oracle.

title: Extract xCore reference tests from xCore.nb documentation notebook
description: Extract reference test cases for xCore utilities from the xCore.nb documentation notebook. Important: resources/xAct/xCore/xCore.nb is a binary Wolfram notebook format. To extract content, either use the oracle /evaluate-with-init endpoint with Get[notebook], or run wolframscript to export it to .wl text format first. Target: at least 20 test cases in TOML format under tests/xcore/ covering all must-implement functions identified in sxAct-0q8. Tests must conform to the JSON Schema from sxAct-67u and must validate against the live Wolfram oracle.
close_reason: 58 TOML test cases across 5 files in tests/xcore/: list_utils (14), symbol_utils (13), dagger_and_registry (14), options (9), upvalues (8). Covers all 34 Category-A must-implement functions: JustOne, MapIfPlus, DeleteDuplicates, DuplicateFreeQ, SymbolJoin, NoPattern, SubHead, FindSymbols, CheckOptions, TrueOrFalse, xUpSet, xUpSetDelayed, xUpAppendTo, xUpDeleteCasesTo, xTagSet, xTagSetDelayed, AppendToUnevaluated, HasDaggerCharacterQ, MakeDaggerSymbol, $DaggerCharacter, ValidateSymbol, LinkSymbols, UnlinkSymbol. All files pass JSON Schema validation.

title: Implement JuliaAdapter stub (returns not-implemented)
description: Implement JuliaAdapter: a concrete TestAdapter (sxAct-hij) that stubs all interface methods by returning a Result with status=not_implemented. Purpose: (1) validates the adapter interface design is complete and all methods are implementable, (2) provides a placeholder that the CLI runner and test harness can exercise end-to-end before Julia XCore exists. Must pass the adapter conformance test suite defined in sxAct-hij.
close_reason: Stubs implemented at src/sxact/adapter/{julia,python}_stub.py; 24 conformance tests each pass.

title: Implement per-file test isolation and lifecycle management
description: Implement per-file test isolation and lifecycle management per specs/2026-01-22-design-framework-gaps.md Section 5.4. An IsolatedContext class manages: (1) fresh adapter initialization per file, (2) running setup actions once and storing handles, (3) scoping store_as bindings correctly (setup bindings available to all tests; per-test bindings do not leak), (4) teardown on completion or failure. Unit tests must verify isolation: a binding from test A is not visible to test B.

title: Design TOML test file format and JSON Schema
description: Design the TOML test file format and its JSON Schema. Reference specs/2026-01-22-design-framework-gaps.md Section 5.1 (action vocabulary) and Section 5.4 (binding scope and isolation semantics). The format must support: setup blocks, test blocks, actions (DefManifold, DefTensor, Evaluate, ToCanonical, Assert), store_as bindings, dependencies between tests, and expected output fields. Output artifacts: tests/schema/test-schema.json (JSON Schema) and tests/examples/xcore_basic.toml (example file that validates against the schema).
close_reason: Created tests/schema/test-schema.json (JSON Schema draft 2020-12) and tests/examples/xcore_basic.toml (6 tests, 5 setup ops). Both files parse correctly and pass structural validation.

title: doc-001: Create initial README.md and CONTRIBUTING.md files
close_reason: Updated README.md to reflect current architecture (oracle/normalize/compare modules, Python package), created CONTRIBUTING.md with setup, test, and workflow instructions

title: Implement oracle snapshot comparison mode (--oracle-mode=snapshot)
description: Implement --oracle-mode=snapshot flag for xact-test run. In snapshot mode, instead of calling the live Wolfram oracle, test results are compared against pre-generated JSON snapshots from the oracle directory. Hash verification ensures snapshot integrity. Must work without Docker or Wolfram Engine running - this enables CI. Reference specs/2026-01-22-design-framework-gaps.md Section 5.6. The CLI runner (sxAct-vho) depends on this ticket.

title: Phase 4: Integration & Validation
description:
## Status: Partially Complete

Write integration tests to validate the full pipeline with real xAct.

## Progress

### Done
- ✅ Created tests/integration/__init__.py
- ✅ Created tests/integration/test_xact_basics.py with 11 tests
- ✅ All tests marked @pytest.mark.oracle and @pytest.mark.slow
- ✅ Documented xAct quirks in docs/oracle-quirks.md

### Test Results: 8/11 Passing

**Passing (8):**
1. TestDefineManifold::test_define_manifold_returns_manifold_info
2. TestDefineManifold::test_manifold_dimension
3. TestDefineMetric::test_define_metric_with_signature
4. TestSymmetricTensor::test_symmetric_tensor_swap_indices
5. TestToCanonical::test_tocanonical_reorders_indices
6. TestMetricContraction::test_metric_contraction_raises_index
7. TestRiemannTensor::test_riemann_exists_after_metric_definition
8. TestNumericSampling::test_numeric_evaluation_of_scalar_expression

**Failing (3) - blocked by sxAct-7g6:**
1. TestSymbolicEquality::test_symmetric_tensor_sum_equals_double
2. TestAntisymmetricTensor::test_antisymmetric_tensor_swap_negates
3. TestBianchiIdentity::test_riemann_first_bianchi_structure

## Blocker

Issue sxAct-7g6: Symbol context pollution breaks xAct tensor operations.
wolframclient parses symbols into Global` context before xAct sees them.

## Remaining Work

1. Fix context pollution (sxAct-7g6)
2. Re-run all tests after fix
3. Verify all three comparison tiers exercised

## Key Learnings

- Use unique symbol names per test (M1, M2, etc.) to avoid protection errors
- Avoid reserved names: N, I, E, C, D, O
- Use SignDetOfMetric instead of SignatureOfMetric
- Multi-call tests fail due to context pollution between calls
close_reason: All 12/12 integration tests passing. Three-tier comparator exercised: Tier 1 (normalized string), Tier 2 (symbolic oracle), Tier 3 (numeric sampling). Context isolation fix from sxAct-7g6 resolved the remaining 3 failing tests.

description: This epic tracks the migration of the `Harmonics` module from Wolfram `xAct` to a new implementation in Julia with a Python wrapper. `Harmonics` provides tools for working with spherical harmonics and other special functions that arise in general relativity and other areas of physics.
acceptance_criteria:

title: Oracle: Symbol context pollution breaks xAct tensor operations
description:
## Problem

When evaluating expressions via wolframclient, symbols are parsed in `Global`` context before xAct sees them. This causes:

1. Tensors like `S[-a,-b]` become `Global`S[Times[-1, Global`a], ...]` instead of xAct tensor expressions
2. `ToCanonical` and other xAct functions don't recognize the expressions as tensors
3. Curvature tensors like `RiemannCD` are created in `Global`` context

## Evidence

```
Context[RiemannCDZ]  → "Global`"  (should be xAct context)
TensorQ[RiemannCDZ]  → False       (should be True)
```

## Impact

3 of 11 integration tests fail:
- TestSymbolicEquality::test_symmetric_tensor_sum_equals_double
- TestAntisymmetricTensor::test_antisymmetric_tensor_swap_negates  
- TestBianchiIdentity::test_riemann_first_bianchi_structure

## Recommended Fix

Implement per-test context isolation:

1. Add `context_id` parameter to `/evaluate-with-init` endpoint
2. Server wraps evaluation in `Block[{$Context = ctx, $ContextPath = ...}, expr]`
3. Pytest fixture generates unique context ID per test
4. All evaluations within a test use the same context ID

## References

- docs/oracle-quirks.md - full documentation of the issue
- plans/2026-01-22-sprint1-derisk-equivalence.md - Phase 4
close_reason: Fixed by implementing context isolation in Oracle. All 12 integration tests now pass. See commit for details.

title: Phase 3: Three-Tier Comparator
description:
Implement three-tier comparison strategy for expression equivalence.

## Comparator Module
- src/sxact/compare/__init__.py
- src/sxact/compare/comparator.py
  - EqualityMode enum: NORMALIZED, SYMBOLIC, NUMERIC
  - CompareResult dataclass (equal, tier, confidence, diff)
  - compare(lhs, rhs, oracle) function

## Numeric Sampling
- src/sxact/compare/sampling.py
  - sample_numeric(lhs, rhs, oracle, n=10, seed=42)

## Three Tiers
1. Tier 1: Normalized string comparison (pure Python)
2. Tier 2: Symbolic diff=0 via oracle Simplify
3. Tier 3: Numeric sampling fallback

## Tests (TDD)
- tests/compare/__init__.py
- tests/compare/test_comparator.py

## Success Criteria
- Tier 1 catches exact matches
- Tier 2 catches T[-a,-b] + T[-b,-a] == 2*T[-a,-b] for symmetric T
- uv run pytest tests/compare/ passes

title: Write tests for Python xCore wrapper (sxact.xcore)
description: Write tests for the Python xCore wrapper (sxAct-caj) at src/sxact/xcore/. Tests must cover: (1) all public Python API functions call through to Julia correctly, (2) Julia exceptions surface as Python exceptions with readable messages, (3) type conversions are correct across the language boundary (strings, numbers, lists), (4) performance overhead is measured and confirmed within 2x of direct Julia execution per sxAct-k0a acceptance criteria. Test file: tests/unit/test_xcore_python.py. Must not duplicate L1 Julia tests - focus on the Python boundary.

title: Design Julia xCore module structure and API mapping
description: Design the Julia module structure for XCore.jl based on the function categorization from sxAct-0q8. Reference resources/xAct/xCore/xCore.m for the source. Output: (1) Julia package skeleton at src/julia/XCore.jl with submodule structure if needed, (2) API mapping table listing xCore function to Julia equivalent or new implementation plan, (3) explicit decision on whether FoldedRule is needed based on xTensor and xPerm usage. The design document lives as notes on this ticket. Implementation tickets sxAct-zaj, sxAct-j4d, sxAct-2nf must not start until this ticket is marked complete.
notes:
## FoldedRule decision: SKIP

FoldedRule, CollapseFoldedRule, DependentRules, IndependentRules are all Category C. Zero references in xTensor or xPerm. They are a Wolfram-specific rule-sequencing idiom with no downstream consumers in the packages being ported. Not implemented in XCore.jl.

---

## Julia module structure decision: FLAT (single module, no submodules)

34 must-implement symbols do not justify submodule overhead. XCore.jl is one module with 11 clearly labelled sections. If section 5 (xUpvalues) or section 8 (extensions) grow substantially during implementation they can be split into sub-files included via `include()`.

Package layout:
  src/julia/
  ├── Project.toml   (uuid 61ed89dc-cc38-478d-be53-e1ee1d7160f1, julia ≥ 1.9)
  └── XCore.jl       (module XCore, all 34 Category-A + 2 Category-B aliases)

---

## API mapping table (Category A)

| xCore symbol | Julia approach | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| SetNumberOfArguments | no-op shim | Julia arity checked at compile time via dispatch |
| AppendToUnevaluated | alias for push\! | Julia has no lazy OwnValues; push\! is equivalent |
| JustOne | custom function | 1-liner with length check |
| MapIfPlus | custom function | dispatch on AbstractVector/Tuple vs scalar |
| DeleteDuplicates | unique (Base) | Category B; order-preserving by definition |
| DuplicateFreeQ | allunique (Base) | Category B |
| SymbolJoin | Symbol(join(string.(args))) | 1-liner |
| NoPattern | identity | no Pattern wrappers in Julia |
| xUpSet | xUpSet\!(prop, tag, val) → Dict | _upvalue_store[tag][prop] = val |
| xUpSetDelayed | xUpSetDelayed\!(prop, tag, thunk) | stores 0-arg thunk |
| xUpAppendTo | xUpAppendTo\!(prop, tag, el) | push\! into Dict-stored vector |
| xUpDeleteCasesTo | xUpDeleteCasesTo\!(prop, tag, pred) | filter\! on Dict-stored vector |
| xTagSet | xTagSet\!(tag, key, val) | delegates to xUpSet\! with :tag_ prefix |
| xTagSetDelayed | xTagSetDelayed\!(tag, key, thunk) | delegates to xUpSetDelayed\! |
| SubHead | SubHead(e) → Symbol | dispatches on Symbol/Expr/other |
| xEvaluateAt | no-op shim | Julia evaluates eagerly; nothing to force |
| xHold | struct XHold{T} | wrapper with custom show; semantics match |
| CheckOptions | custom function | flattens and type-checks for Pair |
| TrueOrFalse | TrueOrFalse(::Bool)=true, else false | 2-method dispatch |
| xTension | xTension\!(pkg, cmd, moment, func) | _xtensions Dict stores hook vectors |
| MakexTensions | MakexTensions(cmd, moment, args...) | iterates and calls hooks |
| ValidateSymbol | custom function | checks xCore/xPerm/xTensor lists + Base exports |
| FindSymbols | custom function | recursive on Symbol/Expr/collection |
| HasDaggerCharacterQ | occursin(DaggerCharacter[], string(s)) | |
| MakeDaggerSymbol | custom function | toggle dagger before $ or at end |
| $DaggerCharacter | const DaggerCharacter = Ref{String}('†') | mutable ref |
| $xActDirectory | const xActDirectory = Ref{String}('') | populated at load time |
| $xActDocDirectory | const xActDocDirectory = Ref{String}('') | |
| $WarningFrom | const WarningFrom = Ref{String}('XCore') | |
| $xPermNames | const xPermNames = String[] | populated by xPerm package |
| $xTensorNames | const xTensorNames = String[] | populated by xTensor package |
| $xCoreNames | const xCoreNames = String[] | populated at load time |
| Disclaimer | custom function | println of GPL text |

---

## Output artifacts

- src/julia/Project.toml  — package manifest (uuid, julia ≥ 1.9, no dependencies)
- src/julia/XCore.jl      — module XCore with all 34 symbols; ready for implementation tickets
close_reason: Julia package skeleton created at src/julia/XCore.jl + Project.toml. API mapping table and FoldedRule=SKIP decision recorded in notes.

title: Optimize xAct loading with persistent Wolfram kernel
description:
Current evaluate-with-init spawns a new wolframscript process for each request, loading xAct from scratch (~3+ min each time).

## Options
1. Use WSTP (Wolfram Symbolic Transfer Protocol) for persistent kernel
2. Pre-load xAct in a long-running wolframscript process
3. Cache xAct initialization (if possible)

## Context
- Current approach: subprocess.run(['wolframscript', '-code', expr])
- Issue: First xAct load takes 3+ minutes
- Impact: Integration tests are impractically slow

## References
- Plan: plans/2026-01-22-sprint1-derisk-equivalence.md (mentions 'keep container running')
- Spec: specs/2026-01-22-design-framework-gaps.md Section 5.1
close_reason: Closed

title: Implement snapshot regeneration workflow (xact-test regen-oracle)
description: Implement the xact-test regen-oracle --diff command that regenerates oracle snapshots and displays a diff before overwriting. Workflow: (1) run all test files against the live oracle, (2) compare new outputs to existing snapshots in oracle/, (3) display changed/added/removed tests with a readable diff, (4) prompt for confirmation before writing. Required for maintaining snapshot freshness when xAct version or Wolfram version changes. Reference specs/2026-01-22-design-framework-gaps.md Sprint 3 item 11.

title: Define adapter interface protocol
description: Define the adapter interface protocol that all CAS implementations (Wolfram, Julia, Python) must conform to. Reference specs/2026-01-22-design-framework-gaps.md Section 5.2 for the full interface: initialize, teardown, execute, normalize, equals, get_properties, get_version. Output: src/sxact/adapter/base.py with a TestAdapter abstract base class. Include a protocol conformance test suite so each adapter can verify compliance independently.
close_reason: Created src/sxact/adapter/__init__.py and src/sxact/adapter/base.py (TestAdapter ABC with initialize/teardown/execute/normalize/equals/get_properties/get_version + EqualityMode enum, VersionInfo dataclass, AdapterError). Conformance suite at tests/test_adapter_conformance.py: 24 tests all pass against DummyAdapter.

title: Implement FoldedRule and rule utilities in Julia
description: Implement Julia equivalents of xCore FoldedRule system if and only if the sxAct-0q8 analysis determines it is used by xTensor or xPerm. FoldedRule represents a chain of rule lists applied sequentially: applying FoldedRule(rules1, rules2) to expr means apply rules2 to the result of applying rules1. Also implement CollapseFoldedRule, DependentRules, IndependentRules, MapRuleR, MapRuleL. Source reference: resources/xAct/xCore/xCore.m lines 562-596. If the sxAct-0q8 analysis determines FoldedRule is not needed by downstream packages, close this ticket with a one-line rationale.
close_reason: FoldedRule is Category C (zero references in xTensor/xPerm) per sxAct-0q8/sxAct-8wd analysis; not needed.

title: Phase 1: Oracle HTTP Bridge
description:
Create Flask HTTP server inside Docker container and Python client on host.

## Server (oracle/)
- oracle/Dockerfile - FROM wolframresearch/wolframengine, install Python + Flask
- oracle/server.py - Flask app with /evaluate and /health endpoints
- oracle/init.wl - xAct initialization script

## Client (src/sxact/oracle/)

title: Epic: Migrate xCore to Julia/Python
description: This epic tracks the migration of the xCore module from Wolfram xAct to a new implementation in Julia with a Python wrapper. xCore is the programming infrastructure and utility layer that all other xAct packages (xTensor, xPerm, etc.) depend on. It provides: symbol registry and validation (ValidateSymbol, $-Names lists), an extension mechanism that lets packages augment Def commands (xTension/MakexTensions), a rule composition system (FoldedRule and utilities), upvalue management patterns (xUpSet, xTagSet, etc.), and assorted programming utilities (argument validation, list ops, option checking, symbol linking, dagger handling). The exact set of must-implement functions is determined by sxAct-0q8. NOT migrated: string formatting utilities (Underline, Overline, TildeString, etc. are Wolfram notebook display only) and the testing framework (xTest is replaced by pytest and Julia Test).
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] All functionality of the `xCore` module is ported to a new Julia implementation.
- [ ] A comprehensive Python wrapper for the Julia `xCore` implementation is created.
- [ ] The new implementation passes all Layer 1 (Unit) tests extracted from the `xCore` documentation notebooks.
- [ ] The new implementation passes all relevant Layer 2 (Property) tests for fundamental tensor algebra laws.
- [ ] The new implementation's performance on Layer 3 (Performance) benchmarks is within 5x of the Wolfram `xAct` baseline.
- [ ] The Python wrapper passes all tests, with performance overhead not exceeding 2x of the direct Julia execution time.
notes:
Acceptance Criteria:
- All must-implement functions (as identified by sxAct-0q8 analysis) are ported to Julia XCore.jl
- A Python wrapper under sxact.xcore exposes all public Julia XCore functions
- Julia implementation passes all Layer 1 unit tests (sxAct-3cv)
- Julia implementation passes all Layer 2 property tests for symbol registry and extension mechanism invariants (sxAct-3cv)
- Layer 3 performance benchmarks pass: execution within 5x of Wolfram xAct baseline (sxAct benchmark ticket)
- Python wrapper passes all wrapper tests with performance overhead not exceeding 2x of direct Julia execution

title: Implement WolframAdapter wrapping oracle client

All existing integration tests in tests/integration/ must continue to pass.

title: Define and run Layer 3 performance benchmarks for xCore
description: Define and execute Layer 3 performance benchmarks for Julia XCore per sxAct-k0a AC (target: within 5x of Wolfram xAct baseline). Benchmark candidates: validate_symbol throughput, make_xtensions hook dispatch latency, FoldedRule application time if implemented. Protocol from specs/2026-01-22-design-framework-gaps.md Section 5.5: 3 warm-up runs, 10 measurement runs, report median and IQR, separate JIT overhead. Store baselines in benchmarks/xcore_baseline.json. Warning threshold: 2x slower than baseline. Error threshold: 5x.

title: Epic: Migrate xPerm to Julia/Python

title: Implement oracle snapshot generation (xact-test snapshot)
description: Implement the xact-test snapshot command: xact-test snapshot <test-dir> --output <oracle-dir>. Runs test files against the live Wolfram oracle and saves results as normalized JSON snapshot files. Reference specs/2026-01-22-design-framework-gaps.md Section 5.6 for the snapshot JSON format and hash computation. Output structure: oracle/VERSION, oracle/config.toml, oracle/<package>/<test-id>.json (containing test_id, oracle_version, timestamp, commands, raw_output, normalized_output, properties, hash), oracle/checksums.sha256.
close_reason: Implemented xact-test snapshot command: runner.py, writer.py, cli.py, 44 tests all green

title: Implement CLI runner (xact-test run)
description: Implement the xact-test CLI runner. Commands: xact-test run <path> executes all .toml files under path; xact-test run --filter tag:<tag> filters by tag. Flags: --oracle-mode=live|snapshot, --format=terminal|json. Exit codes: 0=all pass, 1=any failure. Terminal output shows per-test pass/fail/skip with summary counts. JSON output is structured results. Depends on TOML loader (sxAct-z8g), adapter interface (sxAct-hij), WolframAdapter (sxAct-mhi), isolation (sxAct-5yh), and snapshot comparison mode ticket.

title: Documentation: Differential Geometry Primer for xActCore
description:
This task is to create a document that serves as a primer on the key concepts of differential geometry needed to understand the xActCore codebase. This document will be aimed at new contributors who may have a programming background but need a refresher on the relevant mathematics.

### Topics to Cover:

1.  **Manifolds:**
    *   What is a manifold?
    *   Charts, atlases, and coordinate systems.

2.  **Vectors and Tensors:**
    *   Tangent and cotangent spaces.
    *   Vectors as directional derivatives.
    *   Covectors (one-forms) as linear maps.
    *   Tensors as multilinear maps.

3.  **Tensor Algebra:**
    *   Tensor product.
    *   Tensor contraction.
    *   Abstract index notation vs. component-based notation.

4.  **Metric Tensor:**
    *   The role of the metric in defining geometry.
    *   Raising and lowering indices.
    *   Calculating lengths, angles, and volumes.

5.  **Differential Forms:**
    *   The wedge product.
    *   The exterior derivative.

6.  **Covariant Derivative:**
    *   The concept of a connection and parallel transport.
    *   The covariant derivative of a tensor field.
    *   Christoffel symbols.

7.  **Curvature:**
    *   The Riemann curvature tensor.
    *   The Ricci tensor and Ricci scalar.
    *   The Einstein tensor.

8.  **Symmetries of Tensors:**
    *   Symmetric and antisymmetric tensors.
    *   Symmetries of the Riemann tensor (e.g., Bianchi identities).
close_reason: Created comprehensive differential geometry primer covering all 8 requested topics: manifolds, tensors, tensor algebra, metric, differential forms, covariant derivative, curvature, and tensor symmetries

title: doc-002: Set up documentation website with GitHub Pages
close_reason: Set up MkDocs Material site in docs/site/, mkdocs.yml config, docs optional dep group in pyproject.toml, GitHub Actions deploy workflow

title: Strengthen specs with missing design phases
description:
## Summary

Oracle review of specs against 6-phase design framework identified significant gaps in Phases 1-2 (problem definition) and Phase 5 (tactical design).

## Root Cause

Specs jumped to Phase 5 (Design) without rigorous Phase 1-2 (Describe/Diagnose). This leaves key architectural decisions unjustified and implementation-blocking details unspecified.

## Required Changes

### Phase 1-2: Add Problem Statement & Root Causes

Add section to specs with:
- **Concrete failure modes** (dummy index mismatch, stateful definitions, oracle version drift, symbolic vs string equality)
- **Root cause → consequence table**:
  | Root Cause | Design Consequence |
  |------------|-------------------|
  | Dummy indices differ across impls | Need alpha-equivalence comparator |
  | CAS definitions are stateful | Isolation strategy per-test/per-file |
  | Oracle outputs version-dependent | Pin versions + normalize before hash |
  | Symbolic ≠ textual equality | Three-tier comparison layer |

### Phase 3: Explicit Scope Boundaries

- Non-goals (pretty-printing, UI, packages beyond core trio)
- Oracle policy: ground truth vs math laws when they conflict
- CI/environment: Wolfram licensing, supported versions, local vs CI

### Phase 4: Missing Comparisons

Add alternatives-considered for:
1. **Equivalence strategy**: string vs AST/IR vs semantic (diff→0) vs numeric sampling
2. **Oracle strategy**: golden files vs live execution
3. **Isolation strategy**: per-test vs per-file vs shared session

### Phase 5: Tactical Gaps

1. **Action vocabulary**: canonical list with args schema per action
2. **Adapter contract**: WolframAdapter / JuliaAdapter / PythonAdapter interface
3. **Comparator module**: Normalization + Equivalence (three-tier)
4. **Isolation semantics**: state machine (setup→ops→capture→compare→report)
5. **Benchmark protocol**: warm-up, sample size, JIT separation, variance policy

### Phase 6: Reorder Roadmap

Attack unknowns first:
1. Oracle runner + output normalization
2. Equivalence checker (alpha-equiv, symbolic diff=0, numeric fallback)
3. Then scale extraction and property generators

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Root cause → consequence table added to spec
- [ ] Non-goals section added
- [ ] Alternatives-considered for equivalence/oracle/isolation
- [ ] Action vocabulary defined with schemas
- [ ] Adapter interface specified
- [ ] Three-tier comparison design documented
- [ ] Roadmap reordered to de-risk unknowns first
close_reason: Closed

title: Implement TOML test loader with schema validation
description: Implement a Python loader that reads .toml test files and validates them against the JSON Schema produced by sxAct-67u. Reference specs/2026-01-22-design-framework-gaps.md Section 5.4 for binding scope rules. Output: sxact.runner.loader module with load_test_file(path) returning a TestFile object. Invalid TOML or schema violations must raise descriptive errors with field names. Must have unit tests that do not require the oracle.
close_reason: Implemented sxact.runner.loader with load_test_file, TestFile/TestCase/Operation/Expected dataclasses, jsonschema validation with field paths, and 20 unit tests (all oracle-free). Also fixed oneOf→anyOf bug in test-schema.json.

title: Implement symbol registry and ValidateSymbol equivalent in Julia
description: Implement the Julia equivalent of xCore symbol registry and ValidateSymbol. Source reference: resources/xAct/xCore/xCore.m lines 678-779. Responsibilities: (1) a module-level registry mapping symbol names to owning packages, (2) validate_symbol(name) that throws if the name collides with reserved names or is already registered by another package, (3) register_symbol(name, package) that adds to the registry, (4) Julia equivalents of the dollar-Names lists ($xCoreNames, $xTensorNames, etc.). Unit tests must cover all error branches from the original ValidateSymbol implementation.
